_provenance.stage: Stage 6 — Synthesis (retroactive rebuild from Codex Stage 3)

revision_log: CORRECTED (Stage 3): Act 872 anchor added; '1.2 million riders' coverage figure dropped (methodologies differ; MOHR ~1.2M passage / MOHR FAQ later ~1.64M MDEC 2024).
CORRECTED (Stage 3): i-Saraan participation corrected from '10.7% of self-employed' to 'EPF: 291,743 participants in 2022' with DOSM denominators (2.88M own-account + 489,800 unpaid family workers).

CORRECTED (Stage 3): INCORRECT — '2022 amendment excluded app-based engagement from employer definition' → 'Section 101C creates a rebuttable presumption of employee/employer status'. The amendment did not exclude app-based; it shifted who bears the rebuttal burden.
REPHRASED (Stage 3): MISLEADING — 'Voluntary opt-in schemes typically struggle' (broad generalisation) → specific i-Saraan numbers + World Bank attribution.

CORRECTED (Stage 3): MISLEADING — 'Medical leave and retirement remain unprotected' → 'PERKESO self-employed coverage includes accident-related temporary disability income. The Act does not create general paid sick leave or EPF contributions.'
REPHRASED (Stage 3): MISLEADING — 'The 2025 bill is the first real adaptation' clarified to 'first dedicated gig-worker statute' (not the first legal-policy adaptation generally).
REPHRASED (Stage 3): UNVERIFIED — 'It is a floor, not a ceiling' replaced with Section 7-anchored 'further terms can be negotiated'.
PRESERVED (Stage 3 VERIFIED): Accident SOCSO mandatory; first phase covers workplace injury and disability.
